subject: Ecology
title: Nutrient Limitation
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Nutrient Limitation.

hook.text: A plant can have plenty of sunlight and water but still starve if one nutrient is missing.

buildup.text: Liebig's law of the minimum says growth is limited by the scarcest essential resource.

discovery.text: In most soils, nitrogen or phosphorus is the limiting factor for plant growth.

twist.text: Adding the limiting nutrient can unlock explosive growth—but also trigger algal blooms downstream.

climax.text: Understanding nutrient limitation guides fertilizer use and prevents environmental damage.

punchline.text: Growth depends on the thing you have least of.

quiz.question: What does Liebig's law of the minimum state?
options: Growth is limited by the scarcest essential resource
Plants grow best with maximum fertilizer
